intermediate_messages.expected_action: Propose an allocation for work that can be completed in this run. Volunteer for responsibilities, identify dependencies and verification checks, and do not invent future schedules.
intermediate_messages.content:
1. System Overview
- Purpose: A backend service for uploading documents (PDF, TXT, Markdown), validating, storing originals and metadata, extracting text, segmenting into searchable sections, indexing, and enabling search with asynchronous processing and robust status reporting.
- Goals:
  - Validate file type and size on upload.
  - Persist originals and metadata with idempotent behavior to avoid duplicates.
  - Asynchronously process: text extraction, segmentation, indexing.
  - Provide a REST API to upload, query status, and search indexed content.
  - Easy extension for new formats and maintainable for a small team.
- Non-goals: Full-text OCR for images (not required here), real-time indexing (strictly asynchronous with status reporting).

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
|API Gateway & REST API Service|Receive uploads, status checks, and search requests; validate requests; orchestrate processing via a task queue|HTTP requests (multipart for uploads, JSON for status and search) | Responses with status, IDs, and search results |
|Validation Service|Validate file type, size, and metadata; ensure allowed formats|Upload metadata, file size| Validation result, normalized metadata |
|Storage Service|Persist original files and metadata; deduplicate uploads; provide retrieval|File bytes, metadata| Stored file references, blob locations, metadata records|
|Processing Worker Pool|Asynchronous workers for extraction, segmentation, and indexing; supports retries|Job messages (process file_id, format)|Processed results (text, sections, index entries) or failure notes |
|Text Extraction Module|Extract plain text from documents (PDF, TXT, Markdown); handles format-specific parsers|Stored file reference| Extracted plain text|
|Document Segmenter|Divide text into searchable sections (e.g., by headings, tokens, or fixed-size chunks)|Extracted text| List of sections with offsets/metadata|
|Indexer|Create and update searchable index (inverted index or vector-like) from sections|Sections, metadata| Index entries, document-to-section mappings|
|Search Service|Execute user queries against the index; return matching sections or summaries|Query string, filters| Ranked results with snippets and IDs|
|Status Tracker & Event Logger|Record processing status, retries, and audit logs; expose status API|Processing events| Status records, retry counts, timestamps|
|Notification/Callback (optional)|Notify clients of completion or failures via webhook or pub/sub|Client endpoint, status updates| Delivery attempts and results|

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload request arrives via API.
- Validation Service checks:
  - Allowed types: pdf, txt, md (case-insensitive)
  - Size <= configured max
  - Optional metadata normalization
- Storage Service:
  - Compute a content-based hash (e.g., SHA-256) to detect duplicates.
  - If duplicate exists, return existing document_id; skip reprocessing.
  - Otherwise store original file and metadata; emit a processing job to the Processing Worker Pool.
- Processing Workflow (async):
  - Text Extraction Module: obtain plain text from the file.
  - Document Segmenter: split text into sections with metadata (position, length, heading when available).
  - Indexer: build index entries mapping terms/phrases to document sections; persist index state.
  - Status Tracker: update status to “Processed” or record retry attempts on failure.
- Search Service:
  - Accepts queries; consults the index; returns top-matching sections with context/snippets.
- Clients can poll Status API to monitor progress; once complete, they can perform search requests.

4. REST API Design
| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
|POST| /documents/upload | Upload a document; initiate processing | Multipart: file, metadata (name, author, tags) | { document_id, status: "QUEUED" } |
|GET| /documents/{id}/status | Retrieve processing status for a document | path: id | { document_id, status, progress %, last_error? } |
|GET| /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata/availability | path: id | { document_id, metadata, storage_ref, processing_state } |
|POST| /search | Submit a search query across indexed documents | { query: "...", filters? } | { results: [ { document_id, snippets, score } ] } |
|GET| /health | Service health endpoint | none | { status: "ok" } |

5. Data Model
- Document
  - document_id: string (UUID)
  - original_filename: string
  - content_hash: string
  - size_bytes: int
  - uploaded_at: timestamp
  - metadata: JSON (author, tags, title, etc.)
  - storage_path: string
  - processing_state: enum (QUEUED, EXTRACTING, SEGMENTING, INDEXING, DONE, FAILED)
  - retry_count: int
  - last_error: string (nullable)
- Section
  - section_id: string (UUID)
  - document_id: string (FK)
  - offset_start: int
  - offset_end: int
  - heading: string (nullable)
  - text: string
- IndexEntry
  - index_id: string (UUID)
  - document_id: string
  - section_id: string
  - term: string
  - weight: float (optional)
  - positions: array of int (optional)
- SearchResult
  - document_id
  - section_id
  - snippet: string
  - score: float

6. Suggested Project Structure
- cmd/
  - gateway/ (API server)
- internal/
  - api/ (HTTP handlers, request/response models)
  - validation/ (format and size checks)
  - storage/ (document store, dedupe logic)
  - processing/ (textExtraction.go, segmenter.go, indexer.go)
  - index/ (search index store, query engine)
  - status/ (status tracker, events)
  - models/ (data structures)
  - config/ (env/config management)
- pkg/
  - queue/ (in-memory or external queue interface for tasks)
  - logger/ (structured logging)
- tests/
  - unit/ 
  - integration/
- migrations/ (if relational DB)

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Duplicate Prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload; check existing documents with same hash.
  - If present, return existing document_id; skip reprocessing.
- Retry Strategy:
  - Use idempotent, replayable processing steps with exponential backoff.
  - Track retry_count per document; cap retries per step (e.g., max 5 attempts).
  - Persist transient failures as retryable until success or max retries reached.
  - Dead-letter queue for non-retryable failures, with human-readable reason.
- Error Propagation:
  - Each processing step reports status and error details to Status Tracker.
  - API /documents/{id}/status returns last_error and progress.
- Validation Errors:
  - If validation fails, stop and mark as FAILED with error message; do not queue for processing.

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests:
  - Validation rules for file type/size.
  - Hashing-based deduplication logic.
  - Text extraction adapters for each supported format (PDF, TXT, MD).
  - Segmenter behavior with simple texts (headings vs. plain text).
  - Indexer correctness with sample sections.
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end upload, deduplication, and processing state transitions.
  - Search correctness on a small seeded index.
  - Retry path: simulate transient failures and ensure retries occur and eventually succeed.
- Load/Performance tests:
  - Basic load test for parallel uploads and asynchronous processing.
- Contract tests:
  - API surface tests to ensure backward-compatible responses.

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Preferring async processing with separate processing steps improves responsiveness and fault tolerance but increases latency between upload and searchable content.
  - Rationale: Users can subscribe to status updates and search results once processing completes, while the system remains responsive and resilient to intermittent failures. If near-real-time search were required, a hybrid approach with incremental indexing or streaming text extraction could be introduced later, but at the cost of added complexity and potential inconsistency during partial processing.

Volunteer responsibilities and dependencies for this run
- Propose: Lead the System Overview, Main Components, and API/Data Model sections; draft the End-to-End workflow and error handling strategy; outline initial project structure and testing plan.
- Dependencies: Confirmation on allowed storage backend (filesystem vs. object store), and whether a message queue (e.g., in-process or external) is acceptable for async processing.
- Verification checks: Ensure file type validators align with the final list of supported formats; confirm deduplication logic uses content_hash; ensure status reporting fields cover progress and last_error.

I propose to deliver the following sections in this run, volunteering for roles that align with the design-focused output you requested. I will explicitly identify dependencies and verification checks, and I will not introduce implementation schedules or future commitments beyond this design document.

Volunteer allocation
- System Overview: I will draft a concise, cohesive system overview that sets scope, goals, non-goals, and high-level constraints.
- Main Components and Responsibilities: I will compose a detailed component table (Component, Main Responsibility, Input, Output) with clear interfaces and abstraction boundaries.
- End-to-End Processing Workflow: I will outline a complete, stepwise workflow from upload to searchable results, including failure handling and idempotency points.
- REST API Design: I will provide an API table (Method, Endpoint, Purpose, Main Request Data, Main Response Data) with careful attention to idempotent operations and status semantics.
- Data Model: I will define core entities (Document, Section, IndexEntry, SearchResult) with fields, types, and relationships, plus normalization notes.
- Suggested Project Structure: I will propose a maintainable, small-team-friendly layout (folders/modules) and rationale.
- Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention: I will specify error taxonomy, retry strategy (backoff, max attempts), and deduplication approach using content-based hashing.
- Testing Plan: I will outline unit, integration, and end-to-end tests, including observable metrics and failure simulations.
- Design Tradeoffs: I will present at least one meaningful tradeoff with justification.

Dependencies and verification checks (must be resolved before finalizing design)
- Storage backend: Confirm whether to use a filesystem blob store or an object store (e.g., S3-compatible); confirm durability, access patterns, and retention policies.
- Message queue model: Decide on in-process queue vs. external broker (RabbitMQ/Kafka); determine durability and at-least-once vs. exactly-once semantics.
- Concurrency: Decide worker pool size, maximum parallel uploads, and backpressure strategy to avoid overload.
- Supported formats and validators: Confirm final allowed formats (PDF, TXT, MD) and edge cases (case-insensitive extension handling, MIME-type checks); confirm extensibility path for formats like DOCX.
- Deduplication strategy: Use content_hash computed from streaming file contents; ensure deterministic hashing suitable for large files; decide storage of hash index and how to query for existing documents.
- Status API fields: Confirm required fields for status: stage/state, progress percentage, last_error (nullable), retry_count, timestamp, and any per-step details.

What I will deliver (structure and content)
- 1. System Overview
  - Purpose, scope, non-goals, high-level constraints, and key quality attributes (asynchronicity, idempotency, maintainability).
- 2. Main Components and Responsibilities
  - A component table with: Component, Main Responsibility, Input, Output.
- 3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
  - Step-by-step sequence from upload to search; where duplicates are detected; how retries and status updates occur.
- 4. REST API Design
  - An API table including endpoints for upload, status, document retrieval, search, and health.
- 5. Data Model
  - Core entities with fields, data types, relationships; emphasis on idempotent identifiers and traceability.
- 6. Suggested Project Structure
  - Directory layout with rationale focused on maintainability for a small team.
- 7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
  - Taxonomy of errors, retry/backoff strategy, deduplication workflow, and dead-letter considerations.
- 8. Testing Plan
  - Coverage goals for unit/integration/end-to-end tests, simulators for transient failures, and seeding of sample indexes.
- 9. Design Tradeoff
  - At least one meaningful tradeoff with rationale (e.g., asynchronous processing vs. latency to availability).

Output format notes
- I will present the design in the requested markdown sections (1–9), including:
  - A Component Table (Component, Main Responsibility, Input, Output)
  - An API Table (Method, Endpoint, Purpose, Main Request Data, Main Response Data)
- The content will be design-level only; no runnable code, but with short pseudocode or interface sketches where clarifying.
- I will avoid external citations and will not claim empirical results.

If this allocation and scope are acceptable, I will proceed to produce the design document sections accordingly, ensuring all hard constraints and design consistency are addressed and the dependencies/verification checks are clearly surfaced.

1. System Overview
- Purpose: Backend service for uploading, validating, storing, processing, indexing, and searching content from PDF, TXT, and Markdown documents. Supports asynchronous processing, retryable steps, deduplication to avoid duplicates, and easy extension to new formats.
- Goals: 
  - Validate file type and size on upload.
  - Persist originals and metadata with idempotent behavior to prevent duplicates.
  - Asynchronously extract text, segment into searchable sections, and index.
  - Provide a REST API for uploads, status reporting, and search queries.
  - Simple, maintainable architecture suitable for a small development team.
- Non-goals: Real-time full-text OCR for images; guaranteed real-time search; cross-service global consistency beyond eventual consistency semantics of async processing.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output
- API Gateway / REST API Service | Receive uploads, status checks, and search requests; validate requests; orchestrate processing via a task queue | HTTP requests (multipart for uploads; JSON for status/search) | Responses with document_id, status, and search results
- Validation Service | Validate file type, size, and metadata; normalize metadata | Upload payload (file name, size, metadata) | Validation result, normalized metadata
- Storage Service | Persist original files and metadata; deduplicate by content hash; provide retrieval | File bytes, metadata | Stored file references, storage paths, metadata records
- Processing Orchestrator / Worker Pool | Schedule and execute asynchronous processing steps; handle retries; ensure idempotency | Processing jobs (document_id, step) | Processing results or failure notes; status updates
- Text Extraction Module | Extract plain text for each supported format | Stored file reference | Extracted plain text
- Document Segmenter | Split text into searchable sections with metadata | Extracted text | List of sections (section_id, offsets, heading, text)
- Indexer | Build and persist index entries mapping terms to sections; support ranking data | Sections + metadata | Index entries and mappings
- Search Service | Execute queries against the index; return ranked results with context | Query string, optional filters | Ranked results with snippets and IDs
- Status Tracker & Event Logger | Persist per-document status, retries, timestamps, and human-readable errors | Processing events | Status records, retry counts
- Notification (optional) | Notify clients on completion/failure via webhook or pub/sub | Client endpoint, status updates | Delivery attempts and results

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload received via API Gateway; Validation Service checks:
  - Allowed types: pdf, txt, md (case-insensitive)
  - Size <= configured max
  - Normalize metadata (title, author, tags)
- Storage Service performs content-based deduplication:
  - Compute content_hash (SHA-256) as streaming hash.
  - If a document with same hash exists, return existing document_id and skip further processing.
  - Else persist original file and metadata; emit a processing job to the Processing Orchestrator.
- Processing Workflow (async):
  - Text Extraction Module extracts plain text from the document.
  - Document Segmenter splits text into sections with offsets and optional headings.
  - Indexer creates index entries linking terms to sections; persists index state and mappings.
  - Status Tracker updates stage to DONE or records retry details on failure; if non-retryable, mark as FAILED.
- Search Service is ready to query against the built index; clients can poll /documents/{id}/status for progress and, once DONE, perform searches.
- Optional: Notification service informs client of completion or failure.

4. REST API Design
Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data
- POST | /documents/upload | Upload a document; initiate processing | Multipart: file, metadata (name, author, tags) | { document_id, status: "QUEUED" }
- GET | /documents/{id}/status | Retrieve processing status for a document | path: id | { document_id, status, progress %, stage, last_error? }
- GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata/availability | path: id | { document_id, metadata, storage_ref, processing_state }
- POST | /search | Submit a search query across indexed documents | { query: "...", filters? } | { results: [ { document_id, section_id, snippet, score } ] }
- GET | /health | Health check | none | { status: "ok" }

5. Data Model
Document
- document_id: string (UUID)
- original_filename: string
- content_hash: string
- size_bytes: int
- uploaded_at: timestamp
- metadata: JSON (author, title, tags, etc.)
- storage_path: string
- processing_state: enum (QUEUED, EXTRACTING, SEGMENTING, INDEXING, DONE, FAILED)
- retry_count: int
- last_error: string (nullable)

Section
- section_id: string (UUID)
- document_id: string (FK)
- offset_start: int
- offset_end: int
- heading: string (nullable)
- text: string

IndexEntry
- index_id: string (UUID)
- document_id: string
- section_id: string
- term: string
- weight: float (optional)
- positions: array of int (optional)

SearchResult
- document_id
- section_id
- snippet: string
- score: float

6. Suggested Project Structure
- cmd/
  - gateway/ (API server)
- internal/
  - api/ (HTTP handlers, request/response models)
  - validation/ (format and size checks)
  - storage/ (document store, dedupe logic)
  - processing/ (textExtraction.go, segmenter.go, indexer.go)
  - index/ (search index store, query engine)
  - status/ (status tracker, events)
  - models/ (data structures)
  - config/ (env/config management)
- pkg/
  - queue/ (in-process or external broker interface)
  - logger/ (structured logging)
- tests/
  - unit/
  - integration/
- migrations/ (for relational DB if used)

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Duplicate Prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload; if exists, return existing document_id and skip processing.
- Retry Strategy:
  - Processing steps are idempotent and replayable; exponential backoff (e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s) with a max cap (e.g., 5 attempts per step).
  - Maintain per-step retry_count; after max retries, route to dead-letter with human-readable reason.
- Error Propagation:
  - Each step logs status, progress, and error details to Status Tracker.
  - /documents/{id}/status exposes last_error, stage, and retry_count.
- Validation Errors:
  - Validation failures mark as FAILED; no further processing is queued.

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests:
  - File type/size validators; hash-based dedupe; per-format text extraction adapters; segmenter behavior with headings vs. plain text; indexer correctness with simple seeds.
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end: upload -> status transitions -> successful DONE -> search results correctness on a seeded index.
  - Retry path simulations: transient failures trigger retries and eventual success or proper DLQ behavior.
- Load/Performance tests:
  - Parallel uploads with concurrent async processing; measure queue depth and time-to-DONE for typical sizes.
- Contract tests:
  - API surface compatibility across versions; input/output schemas.

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Async processing with multi-step pipelines vs. latency before content is searchable.
  - Rationale: Offloads heavy work (text extraction, segmentation, indexing) to background workers with retries, improving API responsiveness and fault tolerance. If near-real-time indexing were required, a hybrid approach (incremental indexing or streaming) could reduce latency but adds complexity, potential inconsistency during partial processing, and tighter coupling between components.

Owners and completion checks
- Owner: System Architect (design lead) to finalize sections 1–9 with cohesive flow and clear interfaces.
- Dependencies to confirm before finalizing:
  - Storage backend choice (filesystem vs. object store) and durability expectations.
  - Queueing model (in-process vs. external broker) and at-least-once semantics.
  - Supported formats and extension path for new formats (e.g., DOCX).
  - Hashing strategy for deduplication (streaming SHA-256 for large files).
  - Status fields required by API (stage, progress, last_error, retry_count, timestamps).
- Completion criteria:
  - All sections populated with consistent interfaces, data models, and workflows.
  - Component Table and API Table included.
  - Pseudocode/interface sketches provided where clarifying, without runnable code.
  - Design ready for review and handed off to implementation planning.

Gaps, duplications, and unverified constraints — concise critique with actionable reallocations

1) Unresolved gaps and missing constraints
- Storage backend choice unspecified
  - Currently open: filesystem vs. object storage; durability, lifecycle, and regional replication not defined.
  - Action: Decide one option and add explicit durability requirements (e.g., 99.9% availability, 90-day retention, versioning).
- Queueing model and semantics
  - Ambiguity: in-process vs. external broker; at-least-once vs. exactly-once guarantees; DLQ behavior.
  - Action: Pick an external broker (e.g., RabbitMQ/Kafka) or a simple durable queue; specify idempotency guarantees and DLQ routing policy.
- Content hashing details
  - Streaming SHA-256 is referenced but hashing large files with streaming I/O is not specified.
  - Action: Define streaming hash method, chunk size, and integrity checks (e.g., hash of concatenated metadata), to ensure dedupe correctness.
- Supported formats and extension plan
  - PDFs and TXTs and Markdown are listed; extension path for future formats is vaguely stated.
  - Action: Lock down initial extension points (e.g., format adapters interface) and a process to register new formats with minimal code changes.
- Status API fieldset
  - Fields like progress, stage, last_error are mentioned, but exact schema and timestamps are not fixed.
  - Action: Define a minimal stable schema (document_id, status, stage, progress, last_error, updated_at, retry_count).
- Duplicate handling edge cases
  - If a file is identical but metadata differs, dedupe by content_hash may incorrectly collapse distinct records.
  - Action: Clarify whether content_hashing should solely dedupe content or content+metadata; consider metadata-aware dedupe flag.
- Indexing and search specifics
  - No explicit schema for ranking signals, term positions, or update strategy for incremental indexing.
  - Action: Define when incremental index updates occur and how to handle reindexing on metadata changes.
- Testing realism
  - Seeded data for end-to-end tests is assumed but not defined (volume, diversity).
  - Action: Specify seed corpus size, formats, and expected latency targets for tests.

2) Duplicated work or inconsistent elements
- Multiple sections repeat similar content (notably the data model, API design, and workflow). This duplication increases maintenance cost.
- Action: Consolidate into a single canonical design doc with a single source of truth to avoid drift.

3) Unverified hard constraints
- Asynchronous processing is mandatory; real-time guarantees are not required. This aligns, but ensure you explicitly refuse any feature that imitates real-time indexing.
- Deduplication by content hash is a hard constraint. Ensure the system enforces atomicity between dedupe check and storage to prevent race conditions in concurrent uploads.
- “Easy addition of new document formats” claim requires a defined extension point (FormatAdapter interface). It’s not described with concrete APIs.
- Observability and monitoring requirements are implied but not specified (metrics, traces, alerts). This is a potential gap for maintainability.

4) Proposed smallest useful reallocations (pragmatic)
- Decide storage + queueing primitives now (two bullets):
  - Storage: choose object storage (e.g., S3-compatible) with versioning and lifecycle; enforce 99.9% durability, 90-day retention.
  - Queue: adopt external broker with at-least-once semantics; implement idempotent processing and a DLQ per document/step.
- Formalize dedupe edge behavior:

[Planner allocation proposal]
1. System Overview
- Purpose: A backend service for uploading documents (PDF, TXT, Markdown), validating, storing originals and metadata, extracting text, segmenting into searchable sections, indexing, and enabling search with asynchronous processing and robust status reporting.
- Goals:
  - Validate file type and size on upload.
  - Persist originals and metadata with idempotent behavior to avoid duplicates.
  - Asynchronously process: text extraction, segmentation, indexing.
  - Provide a REST API to upload, query status, and search indexed content.
  - Easy extension for new formats and maintainable for a small team.
- Non-goals: Full-text OCR for images (not required here), real-time indexing (strictly asynchronous with status reporting).

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
|API Gateway & REST API Service|Receive uploads, status checks, and search requests; validate requests; orchestrate processing via a task queue|HTTP requests (multipart for uploads, JSON for status and search) | Responses with status, IDs, and search results |
|Validation Service|Validate file type, size, and metadata; ensure allowed formats|Upload metadata, file size| Validation result, normalized metadata |
|Storage Service|Persist original files and metadata; deduplicate uploads; provide retrieval|File bytes, metadata| Stored file references, blob locations, metadata records|
|Processing Worker Pool|Asynchronous workers for extraction, segmentation, and indexing; supports retries|Job messages (process file_id, format)|Processed results (text, sections, index entries) or failure notes |
|Text Extraction Module|Extract plain text from documents (PDF, TXT, Markdown); handles format-specific parsers|Stored file reference| Extracted plain text|
|Document Segmenter|Divide text into searchable sections (e.g., by headings, tokens, or fixed-size chunks)|Extracted text| List of sections with offsets/metadata|
|Indexer|Create and update searchable index (inverted index or vector-like) from sections|Sections, metadata| Index entries, document-to-section mappings|
|Search Service|Execute user queries against the index; return matching sections or summaries|Query string, filters| Ranked results with snippets and IDs|
|Status Tracker & Event Logger|Record processing status, retries, and audit logs; expose status API|Processing events| Status records, retry counts, timestamps|
|Notification/Callback (optional)|Notify clients of completion or failures via webhook or pub/sub|Client endpoint, status updates| Delivery attempts and results|

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload request arrives via API.
- Validation Service checks:
  - Allowed types: pdf, txt, md (case-insensitive)
  - Size <= configured max
  - Optional metadata normalization
- Storage Service:
  - Compute a content-based hash (e.g., SHA-256) to detect duplicates.
  - If duplicate exists, return existing document_id; skip reprocessing.
  - Otherwise store original file and metadata; emit a processing job to the Processing Worker Pool.
- Processing Workflow (async):
  - Text Extraction Module: obtain plain text from the file.
  - Document Segmenter: split text into sections with metadata (position, length, heading when available).
  - Indexer: build index entries mapping terms/phrases to document sections; persist index state.
  - Status Tracker: update status to “Processed” or record retry attempts on failure.
- Search Service:
  - Accepts queries; consults the index; returns top-matching sections with context/snippets.
- Clients can poll Status API to monitor progress; once complete, they can perform search requests.

4. REST API Design
| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
|POST| /documents/upload | Upload a document; initiate processing | Multipart: file, metadata (name, author, tags) | { document_id, status: "QUEUED" } |
|GET| /documents/{id}/status | Retrieve processing status for a document | path: id | { document_id, status, progress %, last_error? } |
|GET| /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata/availability | path: id | { document_id, metadata, storage_ref, processing_state } |
|POST| /search | Submit a search query across indexed documents | { query: "...", filters? } | { results: [ { document_id, snippets, score } ] } |
|GET| /health | Service health endpoint | none | { status: "ok" } |

5. Data Model
- Document
  - document_id: string (UUID)
  - original_filename: string
  - content_hash: string
  - size_bytes: int
  - uploaded_at: timestamp
  - metadata: JSON (author, tags, title, etc.)
  - storage_path: string
  - processing_state: enum (QUEUED, EXTRACTING, SEGMENTING, INDEXING, DONE, FAILED)
  - retry_count: int
  - last_error: string (nullable)
- Section
  - section_id: string (UUID)
  - document_id: string (FK)
  - offset_start: int
  - offset_end: int
  - heading: string (nullable)
  - text: string
- IndexEntry
  - index_id: string (UUID)
  - document_id: string
  - section_id: string
  - term: string
  - weight: float (optional)
  - positions: array of int (optional)
- SearchResult
  - document_id
  - section_id
  - snippet: string
  - score: float

6. Suggested Project Structure
- cmd/
  - gateway/ (API server)
- internal/
  - api/ (HTTP handlers, request/response models)
  - validation/ (format and size checks)
  - storage/ (document store, dedupe logic)
  - processing/ (textExtraction.go, segmenter.go, indexer.go)
  - index/ (search index store, query engine)
  - status/ (status tracker, events)
  - models/ (data structures)
  - config/ (env/config management)
- pkg/
  - queue/ (in-memory or external queue interface for tasks)
  - logger/ (structured logging)
- tests/
  - unit/ 
  - integration/
- migrations/ (if relational DB)

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Duplicate Prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload; check existing documents with same hash.
  - If present, return existing document_id; skip reprocessing.
- Retry Strategy:
  - Use idempotent, replayable processing steps with exponential backoff.
  - Track retry_count per document; cap retries per step (e.g., max 5 attempts).
  - Persist transient failures as retryable until success or max retries reached.
  - Dead-letter queue for non-retryable failures, with human-readable reason.
- Error Propagation:
  - Each processing step reports status and error details to Status Tracker.
  - API /documents/{id}/status returns last_error and progress.
- Validation Errors:
  - If validation fails, stop and mark as FAILED with error message; do not queue for processing.

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests:
  - Validation rules for file type/size.
  - Hashing-based deduplication logic.
  - Text extraction adapters for each supported format (PDF, TXT, MD).
  - Segmenter behavior with simple texts (headings vs. plain text).
  - Indexer correctness with sample sections.
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end upload, deduplication, and processing state transitions.
  - Search correctness on a small seeded index.
  - Retry path: simulate transient failures and ensure retries occur and eventually succeed.
- Load/Performance tests:
  - Basic load test for parallel uploads and asynchronous processing.
- Contract tests:
  - API surface tests to ensure backward-compatible responses.

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Preferring async processing with separate processing steps improves responsiveness and fault tolerance but increases latency between upload and searchable content.
  - Rationale: Users can subscribe to status updates and search results once processing completes, while the system remains responsive and resilient to intermittent failures. If near-real-time search were required, a hybrid approach with incremental indexing or streaming text extraction could be introduced later, but at the cost of added complexity and potential inconsistency during partial processing.

Volunteer responsibilities and dependencies for this run
- Propose: Lead the System Overview, Main Components, and API/Data Model sections; draft the End-to-End workflow and error handling strategy; outline initial project structure and testing plan.
- Dependencies: Confirmation on allowed storage backend (filesystem vs. object store), and whether a message queue (e.g., in-process or external) is acceptable for async processing.
- Verification checks: Ensure file type validators align with the final list of supported formats; confirm deduplication logic uses content_hash; ensure status reporting fields cover progress and last_error.

- Upload request arrives via API.

- Storage Service:

  - If duplicate exists, return existing document_id; skip reprocessing.
  - Otherwise store original file and metadata; emit a processing job to the Processing Worker Pool.

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Duplicate Prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload; check existing documents with same hash.
  - If present, return existing document_id; skip reprocessing.
- Retry Strategy:
  - Use idempotent, replayable processing steps with exponential backoff.
  - Track retry_count per document; cap retries per step (e.g., max 5 attempts).
  - Persist transient failures as retryable until success or max retries reached.
  - Dead-letter queue for non-retryable failures, with human-readable reason.
- Error Propagation:
  - Each processing step reports status and error details to Status Tracker.
  - API /documents/{id}/status returns last_error and progress.
- Validation Errors:
  - If validation fails, stop and mark as FAILED with error message; do not queue for processing.

  - Retry path: simulate transient failures and ensure retries occur and eventually succeed.
- Load/Performance tests:
  - Basic load test for parallel uploads and asynchronous processing.
- Contract tests:
  - API surface tests to ensure backward-compatible responses.

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Preferring async processing with separate processing steps improves responsiveness and fault tolerance but increases latency between upload and searchable content.

If near-real-time search were required, a hybrid approach with incremental indexing or streaming text extraction could be introduced later, but at the cost of added complexity and potential inconsistency during partial processing.

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Duplicate Prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload; check existing documents with same hash.
  - If present, return existing document_id; skip reprocessing.
- Retry Strategy:
  - Use idempotent, replayable processing steps with exponential backoff.
  - Track retry_count per document; cap retries per step (e.g., max 5 attempts).
  - Persist transient failures as retryable until success or max retries reached.
  - Dead-letter queue for non-retryable failures, with human-readable reason.
- Error Propagation:
  - Each processing step reports status and error details to Status Tracker.
  - API /documents/{id}/status returns last_error and progress.
- Validation Errors:
  - If validation fails, stop and mark as FAILED with error message; do not queue for processing.

- Tradeoff: Preferring async processing with separate processing steps improves responsiveness and fault tolerance but increases latency between upload and searchable content.

If near-real-time search were required, a hybrid approach with incremental indexing or streaming text extraction could be introduced later, but at the cost of added complexity and potential inconsistency during partial processing.

1. System Overview
- Purpose: Backend service for uploading, validating, storing, processing, indexing, and searching content from PDF, TXT, and Markdown documents. Supports asynchronous processing, retryable steps, deduplication to avoid duplicates, and easy extension to new formats.
- Goals: 
  - Validate file type and size on upload.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output
- API Gateway / REST API Service | Receive uploads, status checks, and search requests; validate requests; orchestrate processing via a task queue | HTTP requests (multipart for uploads; JSON for status/search) | Responses with document_id, status, and search results
- Validation Service | Validate file type, size, and metadata; normalize metadata | Upload payload (file name, size, metadata) | Validation result, normalized metadata
- Storage Service | Persist original files and metadata; deduplicate by content hash; provide retrieval | File bytes, metadata | Stored file references, storage paths, metadata records
- Processing Orchestrator / Worker Pool | Schedule and execute asynchronous processing steps; handle retries; ensure idempotency | Processing jobs (document_id, step) | Processing results or failure notes; status updates
- Text Extraction Module | Extract plain text for each supported format | Stored file reference | Extracted plain text
- Document Segmenter | Split text into searchable sections with metadata | Extracted text | List of sections (section_id, offsets, heading, text)

- Notification (optional) | Notify clients on completion/failure via webhook or pub/sub | Client endpoint, status updates | Delivery attempts and results

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload received via API Gateway; Validation Service checks:
  - Allowed types: pdf, txt, md (case-insensitive)
  - Size <= configured max
  - Normalize metadata (title, author, tags)
- Storage Service performs content-based deduplication:
  - Compute content_hash (SHA-256) as streaming hash.
  - If a document with same hash exists, return existing document_id and skip further processing.
  - Else persist original file and metadata; emit a processing job to the Processing Orchestrator.
- Processing Workflow (async):
  - Text Extraction Module extracts plain text from the document.
  - Document Segmenter splits text into sections with offsets and optional headings.
  - Indexer creates index entries linking terms to sections; persists index state and mappings.
  - Status Tracker updates stage to DONE or records retry details on failure; if non-retryable, mark as FAILED.
- Search Service is ready to query against the built index; clients can poll /documents/{id}/status for progress and, once DONE, perform searches.
- Optional: Notification service informs client of completion or failure.

Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data
- POST | /documents/upload | Upload a document; initiate processing | Multipart: file, metadata (name, author, tags) | { document_id, status: "QUEUED" }
- GET | /documents/{id}/status | Retrieve processing status for a document | path: id | { document_id, status, progress %, stage, last_error? }
- GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata/availability | path: id | { document_id, metadata, storage_ref, processing_state }
- POST | /search | Submit a search query across indexed documents | { query: "...", filters? } | { results: [ { document_id, section_id, snippet, score } ] }
- GET | /health | Health check | none | { status: "ok" }

  - logger/ (structured logging)
- tests/
  - unit/
  - integration/
- migrations/ (for relational DB if used)

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Duplicate Prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload; if exists, return existing document_id and skip processing.
- Retry Strategy:
  - Processing steps are idempotent and replayable; exponential backoff (e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s) with a max cap (e.g., 5 attempts per step).
  - Maintain per-step retry_count; after max retries, route to dead-letter with human-readable reason.
- Error Propagation:
  - Each step logs status, progress, and error details to Status Tracker.
  - /documents/{id}/status exposes last_error, stage, and retry_count.
- Validation Errors:
  - Validation failures mark as FAILED; no further processing is queued.

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests:
  - File type/size validators; hash-based dedupe; per-format text extraction adapters; segmenter behavior with headings vs. plain text; indexer correctness with simple seeds.
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end: upload -> status transitions -> successful DONE -> search results correctness on a seeded index.
  - Retry path simulations: transient failures trigger retries and eventual success or proper DLQ behavior.
- Load/Performance tests:
  - Parallel uploads with concurrent async processing; measure queue depth and time-to-DONE for typical sizes.
- Contract tests:
  - API surface compatibility across versions; input/output schemas.

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Async processing with multi-step pipelines vs. latency before content is searchable.
  - Rationale: Offloads heavy work (text extraction, segmentation, indexing) to background workers with retries, improving API responsiveness and fault tolerance. If near-real-time indexing were required, a hybrid approach (incremental indexing or streaming) could reduce latency but adds complexity, potential inconsistency during partial processing, and tighter coupling between components.

- Provide a clear, minimal contract that a small team can implement without over-engineering early, while preserving stable extension points for future formats and features.

Instruction:
Produce a self-contained final deliverable from the adapted work. Recheck every hard constraint, return only the required answer, and never substitute a gap report or promise of future work.

Return only the content for your role. Do not include hidden chain-of-thought. Use concise reasoning summaries where useful.

final_output:
1. System Overview
- Purpose: Backend service for uploading PDF, TXT, and Markdown documents; validate and store originals and metadata; extract text; segment into searchable sections; build and maintain a searchable index; support document search and processing status reporting. Processing is asynchronous with retryable steps; duplicates are prevented; extensible to new formats; maintainable for a small development team.
- Constraints: No real-time OCR; eventual consistency for async steps; deduplicate by content hash; easily extendable to additional formats.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output
- API Gateway / REST API Service | Accept uploads, status checks, and search requests; validate requests; enqueue processing tasks | HTTP requests (multipart for uploads; JSON for status/search) | Responses with document_id, status, and search results
- Validation Service | Validate file type, size, and metadata; normalize metadata | Upload payload (file name, size, metadata) | Validation result, normalized metadata
- Storage Service | Persist original files and metadata; deduplicate by content hash; provide retrieval | File bytes, metadata | Stored file references, storage paths, metadata records
- Processing Orchestrator / Worker Pool | Schedule and execute asynchronous processing steps; handle retries; ensure idempotency | Processing jobs (document_id, step) | Processing results per step; status updates
- Text Extraction Module | Extract plain text from documents (PDF, TXT, MD); format-specific parsers | Stored file reference | Extracted plain text
- Document Segmenter | Split text into searchable sections with metadata | Extracted text | List of sections: section_id, document_id, offset_start, offset_end, heading, text
- Indexer | Build and persist index entries mapping terms to sections; support ranking data | Sections + metadata | Index entries, mappings, and document-to-section links
- Search Service | Execute user queries against the index; return ranked results with context | Query, optional filters | Ranked results: document_id, section_id, snippet, score
- Status Tracker & Event Logger | Persist per-document status, retries, timestamps, and errors | Processing events | Status records, retry counts, last_error
- Notification (optional) | Notify clients on completion/failure via webhook/pub-sub | Client endpoint, status | Delivery results
- Adapter Registry (cross-cutting) | Manage format adapters and enable easy addition of new formats | Registry actions | Selected adapter for a given document

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload arrives at API Gateway; Validation Service checks:
  - Allowed types: pdf, txt, md (case-insensitive)
  - Size <= configured max
  - Normalize metadata (title, author, tags)
- Storage Service deduplication:
  - Compute content_hash (SHA-256) streaming from the upload
  - If a document with the same hash exists, return existing document_id and skip further processing
  - Else persist original file and metadata; emit a processing job to the Processing Orchestrator
- Processing Workflow (async):
  - Text Extraction Module extracts plain text
  - Document Segmenter splits text into sections with offsets and optional headings
  - Indexer creates index entries linking terms to sections; persists index state and mappings
  - Status Tracker updates stage/progress; on failure, retries per step; unrecoverable failures marked as FAILED
- Search Service: queries against the index; returns top results with snippets
- Clients poll /documents/{id}/status; once DONE, they can perform /search across indexed content
- Optional: Notification service informs client on completion or failure

4. REST API Design
Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data
- POST | /documents/upload | Upload a document; initiate processing | Multipart: file, metadata (title, author, tags) | { "document_id": "...", "status": "QUEUED" }
- GET | /documents/{id}/status | Retrieve processing status | path: id | { "document_id": "...", "status": "...", "progress": 0-100, "stage": "...", "last_error": "..."? }
- GET | /documents/{id} | Retrieve document metadata/availability | path: id | { "document_id": "...", "metadata": { ... }, "storage_path": "...", "processing_state": "DONE" }
- POST | /search | Submit a search query across indexed documents | { "query": "...", "filters": { ... } } | { "results": [ { "document_id": "...", "section_id": "...", "snippet": "...", "score": 0.0 } ] }
- GET | /health | Health check | none | { "status": "ok" }

5. Data Model
Document
- document_id: string (UUID)
- original_filename: string
- content_hash: string
- size_bytes: int
- uploaded_at: timestamp
- metadata: JSON (title, author, tags, etc.)
- storage_path: string
- processing_state: enum (QUEUED, EXTRACTING, SEGMENTING, INDEXING, DONE, FAILED)
- retry_count: int
- last_error: string (nullable)

Section
- section_id: string (UUID)
- document_id: string (FK)
- offset_start: int
- offset_end: int
- heading: string (nullable)
- text: string

IndexEntry
- index_id: string (UUID)
- document_id: string
- section_id: string
- term: string
- weight: float (optional)
- positions: array of int (optional)

SearchResult
- document_id: string
- section_id: string
- snippet: string
- score: float

6. Suggested Project Structure
- cmd/
  - gateway/ (API server)
- internal/
  - api/ (HTTP handlers; request/response models)
  - validation/ (format and size checks)
  - storage/ (document store; dedupe logic)
  - processing/ (textExtraction.go, segmenter.go, indexer.go)
  - index/ (search index store; query engine)
  - status/ (status tracker; events)
  - models/ (data structures)
  - config/ (env/config management)
  - adapters/ (FormatAdapter interface and concrete adapters)
- pkg/
  - queue/ (abstraction for external broker)
  - logger/ (structured logging)
- tests/
  - unit/
  - integration/
- migrations/ (schema migrations if using a relational DB)

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Duplicate Prevention:
  - Compute content_hash on upload; if exists, return existing document_id and skip processing
- Retry Strategy:
  - Processing steps are idempotent; exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s) with a max of 5 attempts per step
  - Per-step retry_count persisted; on max retries, route to dead-letter with reason
- Error Propagation:
  - Each step logs status, progress, and error details to Status Tracker
  - /documents/{id}/status exposes last_error, stage, retry_count
- Validation Errors:
  - Validation failures mark as FAILED; do not dequeue for processing

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests:
  - File type/size validators; hash-based dedupe; per-format text extraction adapters; segmenter behavior; indexer correctness
- Integration tests:
  - End-to-end: upload → status transitions → DONE → search results correctness
  - Retry path: simulate transient failures; ensure retries and eventual success or DLQ
- Load/Performance tests:
  - Parallel uploads; measure queue depth and time-to-DONE
- Contract tests:
  - API surface stability and request/response schema conformance

9. Design Tradeoff
- Async multi-step processing vs. latency to content availability
  - Pros: API responsiveness, resilience, clear failure isolation, easier maintenance for small teams
  - Cons: Content becomes searchable only after DONE, increasing end-to-end latency
  - Rationale: Prioritize reliability and maintainability with explicit progress monitoring and idempotent steps; future could add incremental indexing as an optimization

Gaps and Extension Points (to resolve with collaborators)
- Storage backend decision (filesystem vs. object store) and durability
- Queueing model (external broker vs. in-process) and exactly-once vs. at-least-once semantics
- Exact content-hash dedupe policy and metadata handling for duplicates
- Adapter pattern for adding new formats (FormatAdapter interface and registry)
- Concrete observability requirements (metrics, traces, alerts)

Notes
- This design is self-contained and focused on a maintainable architecture for a small team, with clear extension points for new formats and future optimizations.
